note: Bookmarks are BookmarkStart/End pairs. Name must be addressable: no whitespace, no '/[]"', no leading '@' or single quote. Duplicate names rejected at Add. Use --wrap to wrap an existing element (bookmarkStart before it, bookmarkEnd after it). Use --range-paths for atomic split+bookmark at char offsets.

add_modes.positional: Insert bookmarkStart/End pair at parent path. With 'text' prop: wraps a run. Without: empty pair.
add_modes.wrap: Add --wrap <path> to wrap a target element. bookmarkStart goes before the target, bookmarkEnd after it. Supports: Run (inline wrap), Paragraph (bookmark inside paragraph), TableCell (redirect to cell's first paragraph).
add_modes.range_paths: Add --range-paths '/body/p[1][start..end]' for atomic operation: split runs at char offsets and insert bookmarkStart/End around the highlighted region in one DOM pass. Optionally set color/bgColor on the wrapped content.

text.description: optional bookmark-covered text. Without this, only an empty Start/End pair is inserted. With 'text' on positional add: creates a run wrapped by bookmark.
text.readback: not a distinct key — lives on wrapped runs

id.description: OOXML bookmark id (w:bookmarkStart/@w:id). Auto-generated (max existing + 1) by default. Can be specified as a custom integer on Add. Updatable on Set (updates both BookmarkStart and paired BookmarkEnd).
id.readback: numeric bookmark id as stored on BookmarkStart

endPara.description: cross-paragraph BookmarkEnd offset. 0 = same paragraph (default). N > 0 = BookmarkEnd placed N paragraphs downstream from BookmarkStart's enclosing paragraph.
